Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 13 points with 0 lead changes and 15 ties. The game saw 3 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 59%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q3, 4:35, Anunoby (NYK): Anunoby 1' Driving Dunk (18 PTS) (Brunson 3 AST). It swung the win probability by +14.5 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q3, 11:46, Anunoby (NYK): Anunoby 27' 3PT Jump Shot (16 PTS) (Towns 4 AST). It swung the win probability by -13.5 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 15, away team 11.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q2, 6:16: Anunoby 28' 3PT Jump Shot (9 PTS) (Hart 3 AST). That single play moved CLE's chance of winning from 49% to 34%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went NYK's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Harden of CLE. Across 34 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 72 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 10:54, with CLE down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 43%. In other words: very realistic, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q3, 8:34, with the game tilting CLE's way. The play in the middle of it: Mobley Free Throw 2 of 2 (12 PTS).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
